consensus.default_position: Most stable CKD can be managed in primary care using structured monitoring and renal/cardiovascular risk reduction.
key_thresholds: Confirm CKD with persistence for >=3 months
Referral support threshold: KFRE 5-year risk >5% (where applicable)
Urgent escalation for rapid decline, nephritic presentation, refractory electrolyte derangement, or suspected systemic renal disease

summary: Primary care first for stable CKD
Use precise thresholds and progression criteria
Escalate quickly when instability or high risk is present
